type: DEFER
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: Please add these to configure_cache() in dspy/clients/__init__.py, alongside your ttl param: (1) compress: Optional[str] = None -- accepts 'gzip', 'zlib', or None, forwarded as  kwarg to Cache(...) constructor call; (2) cull_limit: Optional[int] = None -- forwarded as  kwarg to Cache(...); (3) eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None -- forwarded as  kwarg to Cache(...). Also update the DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) module-level instantiation to pass compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None (defaults, no behavior change). No new top-level functions needed from me (set_compression, if added, will live as a method on the Cache class in cache.py, not in __init__.py), so no __all__ export needed for my feature. Docstring lines for the 3 new params: 'compress: Optional on-disk compression codec ("gzip", "zlib", or None) for cache values.', 'cull_limit: Optional override for DiskCache cull_limit (max items evicted per cull).', 'eviction_policy: Optional override for DiskCache eviction_policy (e.g. "least-recently-stored").'
note: I own cache.py, deferring __init__.py to you since you're claiming it for TTL

type: DEFER
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: In dspy/clients/__init__.py: (1) Add ttl: Optional[int] = None parameter to configure_cache(), passed through to Cache(...) constructor as ttl=ttl (Cache.__init__ signature, which I own in cache.py, will have ttl: Optional[int] = None as a new kwarg after memory_max_entries). (2) Add a new top-level helper function set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int]) -> None that imports dspy and calls dspy.configure_cache(ttl=seconds) while preserving other current cache settings if easily accessible (otherwise minimal: just re-invoke configure_cache with ttl=seconds and existing other defaults) -- simplest correct approach: read current dspy.cache attributes if present (enable_disk_cache, enable_memory_cache, disk_cache.directory, disk_cache.size_limit, memory_max_entries via memory_cache maxsize) and call configure_cache passing those plus ttl=seconds; if that's too complex feel free to keep it simple by just updating dspy.cache.ttl-related internals directly -- I defer exact implementation detail to you, only requirement: after calling set_ttl(N), new cache entries in both memory and disk layers expire after N seconds, and set_ttl(None) restores infinite life. (3) Update the DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) instantiation to pass ttl=None explicitly (or omit, since default is None) -- just make sure Cache() call is compatible with the new ttl kwarg. (4) Add 'set_ttl' to the __all__ list.

type: SPEC
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: CORRECTION to my previous garbled SPEC (backticks broke shell escaping, ignore that one). Please add these to configure_cache() in dspy/clients/__init__.py, alongside your ttl param: (1) compress: Optional[str] = None, accepts gzip, zlib, or None, forwarded as compress kwarg to the Cache(...) constructor call. (2) cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, forwarded as cull_limit kwarg to Cache(...). (3) eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None, forwarded as eviction_policy kwarg to Cache(...). Also update the DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) module-level instantiation to pass compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None as defaults, no behavior change. No new top-level function needed from me and no new __all__ export for my feature - any set_compression helper will live as a method on the Cache class in cache.py. Docstring additions: compress -- Optional on-disk compression codec, gzip or zlib or None, for cache values. cull_limit -- Optional override for DiskCache cull_limit, max items evicted per cull. eviction_policy -- Optional override for DiskCache eviction_policy, e.g. least-recently-stored.
note: resend of __init__.py spec, clean text this time

type: SPEC
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
spec: In dspy/clients/cache.py please implement TTL: (1) Import TTLCache from cachetools alongside LRUCache. (2) Add ttl: Optional[int] = None parameter to Cache.__init__ signature (after memory_max_entries), documented as 'ttl: Optional default time-to-live in seconds for cache entries; None means entries never expire.' Store as self.ttl = ttl if useful. (3) When enable_memory_cache is True: if ttl is not None, self.memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl); else self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries) (unchanged). (4) In Cache.put(): when writing to disk cache, if self.ttl is not None use self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) instead of self.disk_cache[key] = value; else keep existing self.disk_cache[key] = value. (5) In Cache.get(): on the disk-cache-miss path (i.e. whenever the 'else: return None' branch / general miss handling runs), if self.ttl is not None and enable_disk_cache, call self.disk_cache.expire() to lazily purge expired entries from disk (call this before returning None, or at start of get -- your call on exact placement, just needs to run on the miss path when TTL is enabled). (6) ttl=None must preserve exactly current infinite-life behavior (no behavior change when TTL not configured). Constructor must accept Cache(..., ttl=ttl) as a keyword arg. Thanks!

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Acknowledging conflict: we both claimed cache.py. Resolving by yielding -- you own cache.py (TTL + my compression), I own __init__.py (your ttl/set_ttl spec + my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy). Acking your __init__.py DEFER spec, will implement it plus my own params there. Sending full compression SPEC for cache.py next.

type: SPEC
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
spec: Full compression SPEC for cache.py (you own this file). Cache.__init__: add compress: Optional[str] = None (values gzip, zlib, or None), cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None as new kwargs after memory_max_entries (before your ttl kwarg or after, order does not matter). Store self.compress = compress. Only pass cull_limit/eviction_policy into the FanoutCache(...) constructor call if not None (so diskcache defaults are preserved when omitted), e.g. build a kwargs dict conditionally and do FanoutCache(shards=16, timeout=10, directory=disk_cache_dir, size_limit=disk_size_limit_bytes, **extra_kwargs). Add a method set_compression(self, codec: Optional[str]) -> None that sets self.compress = codec (validate codec in (None, gzip, zlib), raise ValueError otherwise) for dynamic changes without recreating the disk cache. Need , ,  at top of file. Envelope format for on-disk bytes: 4-byte magic b DSPC (literal ASCII bytes D S P C) + 1 byte codec selector (b\x00 = none, b\x01 = gzip, b\x02 = zlib) + payload (pickled, then optionally compressed). In put(), when self.enable_disk_cache and self.compress is truthy: payload = pickle.dumps(value); if self.compress == gzip: payload = gzip.compress(payload); elif self.compress == zlib: payload = zlib.compress(payload); build codec_byte matching selection; store self.disk_cache[key] = b DSPC + codec_byte + payload (a bytes object). When self.compress is None/falsy, keep existing behavior unchanged: self.disk_cache[key] = value directly (do NOT wrap in envelope when compression disabled, for zero overhead and max backward compat). In get(), after retrieving raw = self.disk_cache[key] (or memory cache path, which is unaffected -- memory layer always stores live objects, never apply envelope logic there): if isinstance(raw, bytes) and len(raw) >= 5 and raw[:4] == b DSPC: read codec_byte = raw[4:5], payload = raw[5:]; if codec_byte == b\x01: payload = gzip.decompress(payload); elif codec_byte == b\x02: payload = zlib.decompress(payload); (codec_byte b\x00: payload unchanged); response = pickle.loads(payload). Else (no magic header, e.g. legacy entries or compression was never enabled): response = raw unchanged (existing behavior). This must apply on the disk_cache retrieval branch only, not the memory_cache hit branch. Round-trip must be lossless for both gzip and zlib. configure_cache() in __init__.py (which I own) will pass compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy through to your Cache(...) constructor -- please keep those exact param names (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) in Cache.__init__ signature to match.
note: detailed compression spec for cache.py, please ack

This matches agent2's SPEC(__init__.py) message sent at 12:57:39 which assumed agent1 implements it. Please confirm with a DONE or ACK so we can both start implementing.

type: SPEC
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
spec: Resend (previous had shell-mangling backticks, ignore it). Full compression SPEC for cache.py. Cache.__init__: add compress: Optional[str] = None (values gzip, zlib, or None), cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None as new kwargs after memory_max_entries. Store self.compress = compress. Only pass cull_limit/eviction_policy into the FanoutCache constructor call if not None, so diskcache defaults are preserved when omitted -- build a kwargs dict conditionally and pass via double-star into FanoutCache(shards=16, timeout=10, directory=disk_cache_dir, size_limit=disk_size_limit_bytes, plus the conditional extras). Add a method set_compression(self, codec) that sets self.compress = codec after validating codec is one of None, gzip, zlib (raise ValueError otherwise), for dynamic changes without recreating the disk cache. Add these imports at top of file: gzip, zlib, pickle (as plain import statements, e.g. import gzip on its own line). Envelope format for on-disk bytes: 4 magic bytes spelling D S P C (ascii, i.e. the literal string DSPC encoded as bytes) plus 1 codec-selector byte (0x00 = none, 0x01 = gzip, 0x02 = zlib) plus payload (pickled, then optionally compressed). In put(), when enable_disk_cache and self.compress is truthy: payload = pickle.dumps(value); compress payload with gzip.compress or zlib.compress matching self.compress; store self.disk_cache[key] as the bytes object: magic + codec_byte + payload. When self.compress is falsy (None), keep existing behavior unchanged: self.disk_cache[key] = value directly, no envelope, zero overhead, full backward compat. In get(), after retrieving raw = self.disk_cache[key] on the disk-cache-hit branch only (NOT the memory-cache-hit branch, memory layer always stores live objects unaffected by any of this): if raw is a bytes object of length >= 5 whose first 4 bytes equal the DSPC magic: extract codec selector byte at index 4 and payload from index 5 onward; decompress payload with gzip or zlib per the codec byte (codec 0 means payload is already raw pickle bytes, no decompression); then response = pickle.loads(payload). Else (no magic header present -- legacy entries or compression never enabled): response = raw unchanged, existing behavior. Round-trip must be lossless for both gzip and zlib codecs. Note: configure_cache() in __init__.py, which I own, will forward compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy through to your Cache constructor -- please keep those exact 3 param names in Cache.__init__ so the call sites match.
note: clean resend of compression spec for cache.py, please ack when implemented
